name: Execution Engine

timeline.summary: B-110 hardening pass made Character signal extraction negation-aware and proof-execution aware, prevented false blocks for technical-plan authoring, release-proof execution, credit-safety work, and negated delegation, added modern Codex/Claude model alias coverage, expanded deterministic corpus to 24 cases, and added benchmark summary rates for false allow/block, unknown-pressure handling, stance vectors, noise suppression, intervention precision, and unseen-pressure generalization. Proof: 48 focused Character tests passed, 225 benchmark/corpus/guidance tests passed, 2120 runtime tests passed, validate agent-operating-character and validate guidance-behavior passed, quick agent_operating_character report 1be88ce4ead87770 passed, quick guidance_behavior report 61d3b6a2b1cbe33e passed; hot-path host/provider call counts remained zero.

B-110 Odylith Discipline hardening centralized signal/law policy, ranked open-world affordances, suppressed ephemeral practice refs, tagged Discipline benchmark scenarios to B-110, and proved zero-credit validators plus quick benchmark families with advisory widening at 0.0.

B-110 final browser QA rerun exposed and fixed Compass fixture voice fragility: fake narrator now normalizes governed phrases into plain maintainer wording before standup validation. Exact failing test passed, then final browser matrix passed 158 with 1 skipped after the final governed surface refresh.

B-110 aggressive QA closeout: agent-operating-character validator passed 19 cases with platform integration green and zero hot-path provider/model calls; guidance-behavior validator passed 6 cases and 11 checks; quick benchmarks passed hard gates for agent_operating_character report 70fd94f9eaa6b982 and guidance_behavior report 5ca4596788ef9804; browser regression matrix passed 158 with 1 skipped.
B-110 aggressive QA hardened visible-proof command handling, transcript-safe practice memory, malformed counter validation, and structured refresh result coercion.
B-110 final QA recorded post-sync validators, quick benchmark family proof, and full browser surface regression for Odylith Discipline hardening.

B-110 hardening connected Odylith Discipline proof obligations, live-voice evidence shaping, platform integration validation, and zero-credit benchmark metrics across Execution, Intervention, Memory, and Benchmark.

B-110 source-local closure proof passed after governed sync: character and guidance validators, quick benchmark families, focused unit/CLI tests, 78 browser regressions, and sync check-only are green; full release proof remains gated on pinned dogfood runtime repair and matched proof benchmark.

B-110 Odylith Discipline source-local proof landed: zero-credit validator, guidance-behavior validator, quick benchmark family selection, and Discipline quick benchmark are green; pinned dogfood runtime proof remains blocked by managed runtime drift.

Updated v0.1.11 governance truth for B-096 so Guidance Behavior summaries, Context Engine, Execution Engine, Memory Contracts, Tribunal-ready evidence, the intervention value engine, benchmark validation, Atlas, Registry, Radar, Casebook, and Compass describe one low-latency visible-intervention contract.

Hardened Context Engine to Execution Engine handshake: canonical execution-engine identity now fails closed on historical target ids before snapshot reuse, shared compact snapshots feed packet/runtime/remediator surfaces, execution benchmark gates enforce accuracy, false-admit/false-deny, and guard correctness before latency diagnostics, and sync validation is green for v0.1.11.

Completed B-099 Context Engine and Execution Engine alignment waves for v0.1.11: canonical execution-engine handshake, shared snapshot reuse, Codex/Claude parity, benchmark cost metrics, docs/specs/Atlas refresh, and sync validation.

Here's the honest answer: Odylith is partially grounding this session, but the execution engine is NOT active in the live grounding path.

what_it_is: Constraint-aware execution runtime that materializes task contracts, admissibility decisions, frontiers, closure, receipts, contradiction records, and host-aware execution hints before Odylith acts.
why_tracked: Owns the boundary that turns grounding into governed next-action control instead of soft prompt advice.
